subject: chunk-je-suis-ici / chunk-j-ai-faim / chunk-j-ai-une-question
notes: Pre-chip-canon sentence/clause-shaped registry chunks from L0-L6. Lesson surfaces already atomize them (recap chips are atoms; lint pins j'ai une question as forbidden). Keep as legacy pattern-instances; do NOT replicate the pattern; consider a status/annotation pass later.

subject: chunk-oui status=active
notes: Post-L3 canon treats oui as passive/trap only and no lesson ever produces it, but registry still says active. Candidate for demotion to recognition/passive in a dedicated registry pass (no edit in this audit).

subject: ici / faim unregistered chip surfaces
notes: ici and faim are produced chips with NO registry item (suggestedPieces itemId null), so mastery events cannot attach to an item. A future registry pass should add word-ici / noun-faim deliberately. NOT in this class: une pause resolves to noun-pause (article-wrapped chip, L5 package convention) and ne ___ pas is the frame skeleton of grammar-ne-pas-sandwich.

subject: dormant registry items
notes: Items present but unused by any v1 lesson surface (see item_registry_usage.csv 'NO (dormant)' rows: tu/vous family, noun-idee, sound-liaison, etc.). Harmless, but they predate the factory loop; keep or annotate in a registry pass.

subject: on y va generalization pressure
notes: Approved as frozen formula only. Any future 'on + y + verb' or 'on va + inf' item must be a new deliberate doorway, not an extension of this chunk.

subject: j'y vais chip pressure
notes: Active chunk whose sentence form is excluded from chips by lint. If smoke shows learners need it as a tappable chip, that requires a canon decision (PROTECTED_CHUNKS stays frozen).

subject: negative composition debt
notes: je ne peux pas (composed), je n'y vais pas (deferred), je ne vais pas (never shown) all wait on the ne ___ pas frame. Each new engine re-raises the clause-chip question; the forbidden calibration list is the guard.

subject: m'aider object-pronoun seed
notes: Deliberate future doorway (t'aider/l'aider, me/te/lui donner). Until opened, m' must stay frozen (Il faut m'aider correctly avoided in L15).

subject: infinitive support family growth
notes: verb-aider + verb-aller now form a modal-infinitive set. Keep it a closed, per-lesson-approved set; do not bulk-add infinitives.

subject: est-ce que intonation policy split
notes: L12 wrapper screens: wrapper only. L13: wrapper-required weaves wrapper-only; intonation lives in sayit comparison copy. Consistent after PR #169 fixes; keep this line for L16+.

subject: L13 fill reuses full wrapped-question frame
notes: Est-ce que c'est ___ ? tests yes/no vs where from the answer side; distinct from L12 s03 (wrapper side). No re-teach.

subject: sentence-level fill options
notes: Full-sentence options are recognition choice surfaces (allowed) but are a legacy style; prefer atom/frame options in new lessons unless the discrimination itself is sentence-level.
